design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore expansive open world, complete quests from NPCs via dialogue menu, gather items and magic artifacts, uncover conspiracy around High King Valwyn's disappearance
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Consequence of irreversible actions—killing NPCs can lock players into unwinnable states; releasing catacombs ghouls escalates world difficulty permanently
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Be one of three archetypal heroes (barbarian, knight, valkyrie) solving a kingdom in crisis through exploration and quest completion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unapologetic commitment to console-style accessibility (menu-driven dialogue, simplified interfaces) without sacrificing depth; embraces permanent failure states and consequence-rich world; inspired by Zelda's action-adventure constraints applied to RPG scale

difficulty.checkpointing: Staying at inn resets NPC attitudes (forgetting crimes) but does not fully restore save state; no traditional checkpointing—world persists with consequences
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through exploration and action consequences: players can voluntarily enter catacombs to permanently increase difficulty, or avoid optional bosses; quest completion order is self-directed

progression.upgrade_system: Collection of magic items including teleportation scroll, healing potions, Mjolnir-inspired returning axe; three required magic artifacts tied to conspiracy resolution
progression.unlock_mechanism: NPCs offer quests via dialogue menu; unlocks gated by quest completion and artifact acquisition; hidden areas like city of Treela and dragon cave discoverable through exploration and NPC hints
replayability_hooks: Three distinct hero classes with implied different playstyles
Massive world (13,000 screens) encourages multiple exploration paths
Hidden boss encounters (sleeping dragon in north, giant in Apple II version, hidden city of Treela)
Permadeath consequence system creates emergent narrative variation

world_building_techniques: NPC dialogue reveals conspiracy lore and hints to hidden locations (e.g., serfs mention northern dragon)
Environmental storytelling through explorable open world with 13,000 unique screens
Character selection (three heroes) implies different narrative perspectives on same kingdom conflict
Day-night cycle affecting world state (color changes, NPC availability)

boss_design.philosophy: Optional/discoverable end-game encounters tied to world exploration; encounter difficulty scales with player choices (releasing catacombs escalates ghoul threat)
notable_bosses: Sleeping dragon in northern mountains (eastern edge)
Giant lurking in northwest (Apple II version)
Catacombs ghouls (optional encounter with permanent world difficulty escalation)
Implied final antagonist related to High King Valwyn conspiracy

faction_indicators: Three playable hero archetypes (barbarian, knight, valkyrie) suggest faction alignment
Kingdom power struggle: dukes and barons wrestling for control after High King's disappearance
Barbarian invasion threat as external faction pressure
danger_signals: Killing NPCs locks quests and can create unwinnable game states—permanent faction alienation
Releasing catacombs ghouls permanently escalates world threat level
Irreversible quest consequences force player commitment to chosen path
environmental_storytelling: 13,000-screen world contains discoverable hidden locations (Treela city, dragon cave, catacombs)
Day-night cycle creates dynamic world atmosphere and NPC availability patterns
Monster pillagings and barbarian threats visible through world design and quest hooks
